assertions.expectation: Categorizes notifications by type: auto-upgrade scheduled, new version available, end-of-life/deprecation, security patch
assertions.reason: Response clearly categorizes all four types: security patches/vulnerabilities, end-of-life warnings, auto-upgrade scheduled, and available version updates

assertions.expectation: Assigns urgency levels to each category (security patches = highest, new version available = lowest)
assertions.reason: Response assigns P0 (Critical) to security patches, P2 (Medium) to available version updates, with proper priority hierarchy

assertions.expectation: End-of-life warnings require action: plan upgrade before the support window closes
assertions.reason: Response specifies EOL warnings require immediate upgrade (P0) when <30 days remaining and planning (P1) when <90 days remaining

assertions.expectation: Auto-upgrade scheduled notifications should trigger pre-upgrade checklist execution
assertions.reason: Response includes specific action plans and checklists for auto-upgrade notifications, including planning and execution steps

assertions.expectation: Security patch notifications may require expedited upgrades outside normal maintenance windows
assertions.reason: Response explicitly mentions 'Schedule emergency maintenance window' and 'Within 24-48 hours for critical CVEs' for security patches

assertions.expectation: New version available notifications are informational — use for planning, not immediate action
assertions.reason: Response categorizes available version updates as P2 (Medium Priority) with action 'Evaluate and plan upgrade' for 'Next maintenance cycle'

assertions.expectation: Recommends setting up Pub/Sub or Cloud Monitoring alerts for GKE upgrade events
assertions.reason: Response provides email filtering rules and monitoring dashboard but does not mention Pub/Sub or Cloud Monitoring alerts specifically

assertions.expectation: Mentions GKE release notes as the source for understanding what changed in each version
assertions.reason: Response does not reference GKE release notes as a source for understanding version changes
